Petition For Approval Of Joint Stipulation Regarding Trustees Of The Susan Williams Marital Trust And Dismissal Of Susan Williams Petition For Instructions
Off Calendar Oct-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Andrew Bassack for (trustees) Arnold Kassoy & Joel Faden. Kevin Clune for Susan Williams. Charlotte Ito for Susan Williams. Colin Kelly, Guardian ad Litem for Casey & Peter Armusewicz. Meredith Bushnell (via court call) for Zachary Williams, Zelda Williams and Cody Williams.

Mr. Bassack stated that they have received and reviewed the examiner's notes and that they will file the signed verification and written waivers of notice today. The petition for approval of joint stipulation regarding trustees of the Susan Williams marital trust and dismissal of Susan Williams petition for instructions was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

Trustees Ex Parte Application For An Order Shortening Time For Hearing On Petition For Approval Of Joint Stipulation Regarding Trustees Of The Susan Williams Marital Trust And Dismissal Of Susan Williams Petition For Instructions

Off Calendar Oct-27-2015 Text Ruling

Beneficiary'S Petition For Instructions Per Pc Code Sec 17200 Et Seq Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms.
Off Calendar Jul-29-2015 Continued To Aug-28-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Beneficiary'S Petition For Instructions Per Pc Code Sec 17200 Et Seq Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms.
Off Calendar Jun-01-2015 Continued To Jul-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Wagstaffe, Kevin Clune & Charlotte Ito for Susan Williams. Meredith Bushnell for Zachary, Zelda & Cody Williams. Andrew Bassak for (Trustees) Arnold Kassoy & Joel Faden. Colin Kelley Proposed Guardian ad Litem (GAL).

The Court asked what was left to be resolved. Mr. Bassak stated that he is not sure, that the initial petition sought relief that the premature, that 6 of the 8 requests for relief are now moot, that the other 2 requests are in regards to the funding of the reserve trust but he is not sure if this is the only remaining issue, that he has heard there may be an effort to unseat the trustees and that he believes the supplemental report filed Friday may be disputes regarding the personal property. Ms. Bushnell reported that her clients will abide by the trustee regarding all personal property, that the setting of deadlines and dates was helpful, that the petition for instructions was not proper, that there are still 300 items in Susan's possession even though they belong to her clients and that the additional appraisal and late request for documents is a delay tactic. Mr. Bassak reported that appraisals have been received on all of the properties and that Susan is obtaining an additional appraisal on the Tiburon property. Mr. Wagstaffe stated that the discretion of the trustee is not absolute, that the parties have been trying to resolve outstanding issues, that tens of thousands of items have been taken from the house and that some of the items were very sentimental to Susan. Mr. Wagstaffe further stated that the reserve account is a key issue before the court, that they expect to have a counter appraisal in the next week, cited 16061, that they have asked for additional information, that they are moving forward in an attempt to resolve the issues, that the reserve is not funded yet, that a GAL has just been appointed, that Susan's children are 2/5 beneficiaries in the Tiburon house upon her death and that they will continue to cooperate and will exchange appraisal information. The Court asked what will happen if the appraisal is dramatically different and what would assist the parties with funding. Mr. Wagstaffe stated that the parties are not that far apart. Mr. Bassak stated that the contested items and adequate funding are intertwined and will be handled concurrently. Mr. Wagstaffe requested a 30 day continuance to exchange information and stated that he is prepared to submit an amended petition if necessary. The Beneficiary's Petition for Instructions was continued to July 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to exchange appraisal information and to have a further session with the mediator if necessary. If by July 17, 2015 resolution has not occurred the parties are to contact the court regarding a new date and to file an amended petition.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Off Calendar

Beneficiary'S Petition For Instructions Per Pc Code Sec 17200 Et Seq Regarding Interpretation Of Trust Terms.
Off Calendar Mar-30-2015 Continued To Jun-01-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Wagstaffe & Kevin Clune for (petitioner) Susan Williams. Meredith Bushnell for (objectors) Zachary, Zelda & Cody Williams. Andrew Bassak & Veronica Cerruti for (trustees) Arnold Kassoy & Joel Faden.

Mr. Bassak stated that there is no reason for the estate to be before this court and that the estate plan was prepared in order to stay out of the public eye. Mr. Bassak reported that the trustees have been trusted with discretion on distribution of the personal tangible property, that the estate tax is due, that the disposition of the tangible personal property needs to be resolved by then and that the trustee's are hesitant to help negotiate agreements between the parties. Ms. Bushnell stated that she is in agreement with the trustees, that it is excruciating to have this matter be public and that her clients need to be able to move on. Mr. Wagstaffe stated that he is in receipt of the final inventory consisting of about 1200 items, that there is a dispute as to some of the items actually belonging to Susan, that the Tiburon house remains available to her in trust as well as a residual amount of money to maintain said house and requested a 6 to 8 week continuance for the parties to meet and work things out. Mr. Bassak stated his agreement to meet and discuss distribution, does not see a need for this petition to continue, states that a petition regarding breach of duties could be filed at a later date if necessary and that the issue of instructions is within the trustees discretion. Ms. Bushnell stated that Mr. Williams did not intend for court oversight, that the trustees are doing a good job, that further delay is not appropriate and that Susan's trust is a separate issue. The court stated that it is inclined to continue the petition to give time for an informal meeting (to take place by 4/10/15), private mediation (to take place in late April/early May)and then allow the parties to come back if there are remaining issues. The petition for instructions, etc., was continued to June 1, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Bassak is to prepare a proposed order outlining the dates.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
